assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and curl or an equivalent HTTP client.
No Telnyx account, numbers, messaging profile, or API key exists yet.

developer_goal: Create a Telnyx account and send a first on-net SMS with the Messaging API.

first_success.outcome: A POST to /v2/messages with a Bearer API key queues an SMS between two Telnyx numbers.
first_success.observable_signal: The Messaging API accepts the send request for the documented Hello, world! text between the two configured numbers.

route_selection.surface: Telnyx Mission Control Portal plus Messaging API quickstart
route_selection.rule: Use the official Send Your First Message guide as the documented self-service messaging route; leave the path unverified while signup fields remain opaque.

candidates.name: Mission Control: buy two numbers, messaging profile, API key, on-net SMS
candidates.reason: This is the official five-minute messaging guide, but account creation and any KYC or billing prompts are not publicly reconstructed.

candidates.name: Off-net messaging to an external carrier number
candidates.reason: The guide states off-net US messaging typically requires sender registration such as 10DLC.

required_path.action: Create a free Telnyx account and sign in to Mission Control.

required_fields.label: Signup and KYC fields
required_fields.notes: The guide requires an account and links signup, but does not enumerate interactive fields.

required_path.observable_result: The developer can open Mission Control portal resources.

required_path.action: Search for SMS-capable numbers and purchase two Telnyx numbers.
required_path.observable_result: Two Telnyx numbers are available in My Numbers.

required_path.action: Create a messaging profile and assign both numbers to it.
required_path.observable_result: Both numbers are assigned to the messaging profile.

required_path.action: Copy or create an API key in Mission Control.
required_path.observable_result: The developer has a Bearer token for Messaging API calls.

required_path.action: POST to https://api.telnyx.com/v2/messages with from, to, and text set for the two Telnyx numbers.
required_path.observable_result: The API returns a successful outbound message payload.

prerequisites.description: Two Telnyx numbers are required so the guide can test on-net messaging without carrier registration.

external_gates.description: Purchasing numbers may incur Mission Control charges depending on number type and region.

platform_outcomes.description: Telnyx accepts and queues the outbound message after a valid authenticated send request.

excluded.item: 10DLC or other sender registration for off-net destinations
excluded.reason: The selected guide intentionally uses Telnyx-to-Telnyx numbers to avoid carrier registration.
Webhooks are outside the first accepted send request.

sources.title: Telnyx sign up
evidence_supported: Official signup entry linked from messaging docs; interactive fields not fully public

sources.title: Create and Manage API Keys
evidence_supported: Mission Control API key creation flow

uncertainties.question: What exact signup fields, KYC prompts, payment-method requirements, and post-submit screens appear for a free Telnyx account?
uncertainties.impact: Account creation remains one opaque step, so required field and gate counts cannot be verified.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated fresh-account Mission Control signup walkthrough including any identity or billing gates before number purchase.

uncertainties.question: Does purchasing the two guide numbers currently require a funded balance or card before Place Order succeeds?
uncertainties.impact: A payment gate would change lexicographic ranking versus any alternate no-purchase messaging demo if one exists.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Official billing docs or a fresh-account number-purchase attempt showing whether payment is required for the on-net test path.
